reportingFrame.applicability: Health estimates across time or space using GBD Results Tool outputs.
reportingFrame.reviewStatus: The profile is ready for manuscript-level human review against GATHER-style transparency expectations, but not final submission.

metricPolicy.rateCountPercentMixingBoundary: Counts, rates, percentages, and age-standardized estimates must not be mixed in one claim unless the transformation is explicitly defined.

ageStandardization.evidence: The selected query uses All ages rows from the GBD Results Tool default endpoint. The manuscript candidate claims must describe these as all-age estimates and must not label the rate as age-standardized.
ageStandardization.allAgesInterpretationRule: All ages is a GBD age category, not an age-standardized estimate. Any age-standardized claim requires explicit age-standardized rows and separate claim registry entries.
ageStandardization.boundary: Do not compare all-age rates with age-standardized rates, and do not interpret all-age rates as age-adjusted.

uncertaintyIntervalPolicy.narrativePolicy: Manuscript candidate claims must report uncertainty intervals as UI lower-upper values from the selected GBD export rows. The wording must avoid p-values, confidence intervals, causal certainty, or model precision claims unless those are separately supported.
uncertaintyIntervalPolicy.reviewReadyRule: For each numeric GBD claim, the registry must identify the exact source row and whether lower/upper values are carried into the prose.
uncertaintyIntervalPolicy.submissionBoundary: Submission-ready GBD claims must keep UI interpretation consistent with the selected measure, metric, year, and age category, and must undergo journal-specific wording review.

citationPolicy.source: Institute for Health Metrics and Evaluation (IHME), Global Health Data Exchange (GHDx), GBD Results Tool
citationPolicy.manuscriptCitationDraft: Institute for Health Metrics and Evaluation (IHME). GBD Results Tool. Global Health Data Exchange. GBD 2023, version_id 8352. Accessed 2026-06-11. https://vizhub.healthdata.org/gbd-results/
citationPolicy.finalSubmissionRequirement: Before submission, confirm the journal's required citation format and IHME/GHDx data-use wording; replace this draft if the GBD export page provides a release-specific citation.

reuseBoundary.rule: The committed small export may be used for repository review and manuscript drafting only while citation and data-use links are recorded. Do not redistribute larger exports, package derivative datasets for release, or claim unrestricted reuse until IHME/GHDx terms are manually confirmed.
reuseBoundary.repositoryPolicy: Keep only small, provenance-tracked exports and derived tables in the repository. Larger or restricted exports belong outside git and should be regenerated or attached as approved release artifacts only when terms permit.
reuseBoundary.submissionBoundary: Submission-ready status requires human confirmation that the exact export, derived tables, and manuscript wording comply with IHME/GHDx and journal requirements.

blockingRules: Missing GBD release or version_id blocks real manuscript claims.
Missing lower/upper source fields blocks uncertainty-aware GBD claims.
Metric type ambiguity blocks rate/count/percent comparisons.
Missing citation or reuse boundary blocks submission packaging.
